stories.title: Approval Plugin Infrastructure
stories.description: Create the foundational WebVella ERP plugin infrastructure for the multi-level approval workflow system. This story establishes the ApprovalPlugin class inheriting from ErpPlugin, the project file configuration, plugin settings model, and migration orchestration scaffolding. The plugin follows the established pattern from existing plugins like WebVella.Erp.Plugins.Project, implementing Initialize(), ProcessPatches(), and SetSchedulePlans() methods. This infrastructure provides the foundation for all subsequent approval workflow components including entity migrations, services, hooks, and background jobs.
stories.businessValue: Establishes the modular, maintainable foundation for the approval workflow system following WebVella ERP's proven plugin architecture. Enables isolated deployment, testing, and versioning of approval functionality. Provides standardized extension points for entity migrations, schedule plans, and service registration.
acceptanceCriteria: ApprovalPlugin class inherits from ErpPlugin and implements IPlugin interface with correct Initialize(IServiceProvider) method signature
Plugin assembly is discoverable by WebVella ERP plugin scanner through correct naming convention (WebVella.Erp.Plugins.Approval)
ProcessPatches() method orchestrates migration patches in correct date-based order
SetSchedulePlans() method stub is available for future background job scheduling
PluginSettings model class is created with IsInstalled property for tracking plugin state

classes: ApprovalPlugin : ErpPlugin - Main plugin class implementing Initialize(), ProcessPatches(), SetSchedulePlans()
PluginSettings - Configuration model with IsInstalled property and JSON serialization
integrationPoints: WebVella.Erp.ErpPlugin - Base class providing plugin lifecycle hooks
WebVella.Erp.Database.DbContext - Database transaction management for migrations
WebVella.Erp.Api.SecurityContext - System scope for plugin operations
technicalDetails.technicalApproach: Create a new .NET 9.0 class library project following the WebVella.Erp.Plugins.Project structure. The ApprovalPlugin.cs file contains the main class inheriting from ErpPlugin with Initialize() method for service registration and dependency injection setup. ApprovalPlugin._.cs contains the ProcessPatches() orchestration method calling date-stamped migration files. The PluginSettings model tracks installation state using JSON serialization for storage in the plugin_data system entity.

stories.title: Approval Entity Schema and Data Model
stories.description: Define and implement the complete data model for the approval workflow system through five core entities: approval_workflow (workflow definitions), approval_step (approval hierarchy levels), approval_rule (routing conditions), approval_request (active approval instances), and approval_history (audit trail). This story creates the migration patch that uses EntityManager and EntityRelationManager APIs to create entities with appropriate fields, relations, and indexes. The entity schema supports multi-level approval chains, threshold-based routing, parallel and sequential approval patterns, and comprehensive audit logging.
stories.businessValue: Provides the persistent data foundation for all approval workflow operations. The entity design enables flexible workflow configuration supporting various business approval patterns. The approval_history entity ensures complete audit trail compliance for regulatory requirements. Proper indexing and relations enable efficient querying for approval dashboards and reports.
acceptanceCriteria: approval_workflow entity is created with fields: id (GUID), name (text), target_entity (text), is_enabled (boolean), created_on (datetime), created_by (GUID FK to user)

approval_step entity is created with fields: id, workflow_id (FK), step_order (int), approver_type (text), approver_id (GUID), threshold_min (decimal), threshold_max (decimal), timeout_hours (int), auto_approve_conditions (text)
approval_rule entity is created with fields: id, workflow_id (FK), field_name (text), operator (text), threshold_value (decimal), next_step_id (FK)

approval_request entity is created with fields: id, source_record_id (GUID), source_entity (text), workflow_id (FK), current_step_id (FK), status (text enum), created_on, created_by, last_notification_sent, notification_count, is_archived, archived_on
approval_history entity is created with fields: id, request_id (FK), action_type (text), performed_by (GUID FK), performed_on (datetime), comments (text), previous_status, new_status
OneToMany relations are established: workflow->steps, workflow->rules, workflow->requests, request->history
Migration patch follows naming convention ApprovalPlugin.YYYYMMDD.cs and executes within DbContext.Current.Transaction scope

classes: ApprovalPlugin partial class - Migration patch with Patch20260115() method
Entity definitions using InputEntity and InputField API models
integrationPoints: WebVella.Erp.Api.EntityManager - CreateEntity(), ReadEntity() for entity operations
WebVella.Erp.Database.DbContext - Transaction scope for atomic migrations
technicalDetails.technicalApproach: Create a migration patch class as a partial of ApprovalPlugin with date-stamped method Patch20260115(). The method first checks if entities exist to support idempotent execution. Entity creation uses InputEntity with appropriate fields defined through InputGuidField, InputTextField, InputNumberField, InputDateTimeField, InputCheckboxField classes. Relations use EntityRelationManager.Create() with EntityRelationType.OneToMany. All operations execute within DbContext.Current.Transaction for atomicity. The patch is called from ProcessPatches() in priority order.

dependencies.description: Plugin Infrastructure - Required for ApprovalPlugin class and ProcessPatches() orchestration

stories.title: Workflow Configuration Management Services
stories.description: Implement the configuration management layer for approval workflows through three specialized services: WorkflowConfigService (workflow CRUD operations), StepConfigService (approval step management), and RuleConfigService (routing rule configuration). These services provide the business logic for administrators to create, modify, and validate approval workflow definitions. The services implement validation rules ensuring step order integrity, threshold overlap detection, and circular reference prevention. All operations are exposed through the services for consumption by the REST API (STORY-007) and UI components (STORY-008).
stories.businessValue: Enables administrators to configure approval workflows without developer intervention or direct database manipulation. Built-in validation prevents misconfiguration that could cause approval routing failures. Service abstraction enables consistent configuration access from both API and background job contexts.
acceptanceCriteria: WorkflowConfigService provides CreateWorkflow(), GetWorkflow(), GetWorkflowsForEntity(), UpdateWorkflow(), DeleteWorkflow() methods
StepConfigService provides AddStep(), UpdateStep(), DeleteStep(), ReorderSteps() methods with step_order integrity validation
RuleConfigService provides AddRule(), UpdateRule(), DeleteRule() methods with threshold validation
DeleteWorkflow() validates no pending approval_request records exist before deletion
All services use EQL queries with proper relation expansion for efficient data retrieval
Validation errors throw descriptive exceptions suitable for API error responses

classes: WorkflowConfigService - Workflow CRUD with validation and EQL queries
StepConfigService - Step management with ordering logic
RuleConfigService - Rule configuration with threshold validation
technicalDetails.technicalApproach: Create service classes following the BaseService pattern from WebVella.Erp.Plugins.Project. Each service encapsulates entity-specific operations with RecordManager for CRUD and EqlCommand for complex queries. WorkflowConfigService includes GetWorkflowsForEntity() using EQL WHERE clause filtering. StepConfigService maintains step_order integrity through ReorderSteps() method that renumbers all steps when one is moved. RuleConfigService validates threshold_min < threshold_max and prevents overlapping ranges. All services return EntityRecord objects for consistency with WebVella patterns.

dependencies.description: Plugin Infrastructure - Required for service registration
Entity Schema - Required entities must exist before services can operate

stories.title: Approval Service Layer and Business Logic
stories.description: Implement the core business logic layer for approval workflow operations through four essential services: ApprovalWorkflowService (workflow lifecycle management), ApprovalRouteService (routing and approver resolution), ApprovalRequestService (request lifecycle and actions), and ApprovalHistoryService (audit trail management). These services encapsulate all approval business rules including multi-step workflow progression, threshold-based routing logic, approver authorization validation, delegation handling, and comprehensive audit logging. The services follow the established WebVella ERP service patterns with RecordManager integration and SecurityContext awareness.
stories.businessValue: Centralizes all approval business logic in testable, reusable service classes. Ensures consistent approval behavior across all access points (API, UI, background jobs). Provides complete audit trail for compliance and troubleshooting. Enables complex routing scenarios including parallel approvals, escalation paths, and delegation chains.
acceptanceCriteria: ApprovalWorkflowService provides GetAllWorkflows(), GetWorkflow(), CreateWorkflow(), UpdateWorkflow(), DeleteWorkflow() methods with admin permission validation
ApprovalRouteService provides EvaluateNextStep(), GetApproversForStep(), ResolveApproversByType() methods implementing threshold and role-based routing
ApprovalRequestService provides CreateRequest(), GetRequest(), GetPendingRequestsForUser(), ApproveRequest(), RejectRequest(), DelegateRequest() methods with authorization validation
ApprovalHistoryService provides LogApprovalAction(), GetRequestHistory() methods capturing all state transitions
ApproveRequest() advances workflow to next step or completes request based on ApprovalRouteService evaluation
RejectRequest() transitions request to Rejected status and logs rejection reason
DelegateRequest() reassigns current step approver and logs delegation action
All state changes create approval_history records with previous_status, new_status, performed_by, and comments

classes: ApprovalWorkflowService - Workflow CRUD with permission checks
ApprovalRouteService - Routing logic and approver resolution
ApprovalRequestService - Request lifecycle management
ApprovalHistoryService - Audit trail operations
technicalDetails.technicalApproach: Create service classes inheriting from BaseService pattern. ApprovalWorkflowService wraps WorkflowConfigService with additional permission validation using SecurityContext.CurrentUser role checks. ApprovalRouteService implements EvaluateNextStep() using EQL query against approval_rule entity matching field values, then returns next step or null if workflow complete. GetApproversForStep() resolves approver_type (user, role, department_head) to list of user IDs. ApprovalRequestService orchestrates the approval flow: ApproveRequest() calls RouteService.EvaluateNextStep(), updates request status/step, and calls HistoryService.LogApprovalAction(). All operations use RecordManager within DbContext transaction scope.

dependencies.description: Plugin Infrastructure - Required for service registration
Entity Schema - Required entities for data operations
Configuration Services - Used by ApprovalWorkflowService for workflow CRUD

stories.title: Approval Hooks Integration for Entity Events
stories.description: Implement pre and post record hooks that integrate approval workflow processing with entity CRUD operations. This story creates hook classes that automatically initiate approval workflows when target entities (purchase_order, expense_request) are created, and trigger notifications and state updates when approval_request records are modified. The hooks follow the WebVella ERP hook pattern with HookAttachmentAttribute for entity binding and implement IErpPreCreateRecordHook and IErpPostUpdateRecordHook interfaces.
stories.businessValue: Enables automatic workflow initiation without manual intervention or custom code in each entity's CRUD operations. Ensures approval requirements are enforced consistently across all entry points (UI, API, imports). Triggers real-time notifications to approvers when their action is required. Maintains data integrity by preventing unauthorized record modifications during pending approval.
acceptanceCriteria: ApprovalRequestPreCreateHook implements IErpPreCreateRecordHook and initializes workflow routing by calling ApprovalRouteService.EvaluateNextStep()
ApprovalRequestPostUpdateHook implements IErpPostUpdateRecordHook and triggers notifications when status changes to pending at new step
PurchaseOrderApprovalHook implements IErpPreCreateRecordHook attached to purchase_order entity, creating approval_request when record is created
ExpenseRequestApprovalHook implements IErpPreCreateRecordHook attached to expense_request entity, creating approval_request when record is created
All hooks use HookAttachmentAttribute with correct entity_name for automatic binding
Target entity hooks check for active workflow configuration before creating approval_request
Hook execution uses DbContext.Current.Transaction to ensure atomicity with triggering operation

classes: ApprovalRequestPreCreateHook : IErpPreCreateRecordHook - Initialize routing on request creation
ApprovalRequestPostUpdateHook : IErpPostUpdateRecordHook - Trigger notifications on status change
PurchaseOrderApprovalHook : IErpPreCreateRecordHook - Initiate workflow for purchase orders
ExpenseRequestApprovalHook : IErpPreCreateRecordHook - Initiate workflow for expense requests
technicalDetails.technicalApproach: Create hook classes implementing appropriate interfaces with HookAttachmentAttribute specifying entity_name. ApprovalRequestPreCreateHook.OnPreCreateRecord() validates record data, calls RouteService.EvaluateNextStep() to set initial current_step_id, and sets status to 'Pending'. ApprovalRequestPostUpdateHook.OnPostUpdateRecord() compares old and new status values, triggering notification queue when status changes. PurchaseOrderApprovalHook first queries WorkflowService.GetWorkflowsForEntity('purchase_order'), then creates approval_request record via RequestService.CreateRequest(). All hooks access the execution context through HookArgument parameter providing record data and controller context.

dependencies.description: Service Layer - Hooks depend on ApprovalRouteService, ApprovalRequestService, ApprovalWorkflowService for business logic

stories.title: Notification and Escalation Background Jobs
stories.description: Implement scheduled background jobs for approval workflow automation: ProcessApprovalNotificationsJob (sends pending approval notifications), ProcessApprovalEscalationsJob (escalates overdue approvals), and CleanupExpiredApprovalsJob (archives completed approvals). The jobs follow the WebVella ERP ErpJob pattern with JobAttribute for discovery and SchedulePlan registration for automated execution. Jobs integrate with the Mail plugin for email delivery and use SecurityContext.OpenSystemScope() for system-level operations.
stories.businessValue: Ensures approvers are promptly notified of pending requests without manual intervention. Automatic escalation prevents workflow bottlenecks when approvers are unavailable. Cleanup job maintains database performance by archiving historical records while preserving audit trail. SLA enforcement through timeout-based escalation improves approval cycle times.
acceptanceCriteria: ProcessApprovalNotificationsJob executes every 5 minutes, querying pending approval_request records and sending email notifications to resolved approvers via Mail plugin
ProcessApprovalEscalationsJob executes every 30 minutes, identifying requests exceeding step timeout_hours and escalating to next step or marking as Escalated
CleanupExpiredApprovalsJob executes daily, archiving approval_request records older than retention period (365 days) by setting is_archived flag
All jobs inherit from ErpJob and are decorated with [Job] attribute specifying unique GUID, name, enabled status, and priority
SetSchedulePlans() in ApprovalPlugin registers SchedulePlan entries for each job with appropriate intervals/schedules
Jobs execute within SecurityContext.OpenSystemScope() for system-level permissions
Notification job tracks last_notification_sent to prevent duplicate sends
Escalation job logs all escalation actions to approval_history via ApprovalHistoryService

classes: ProcessApprovalNotificationsJob : ErpJob - Notification dispatch every 5 minutes
ProcessApprovalEscalationsJob : ErpJob - Overdue request escalation every 30 minutes
CleanupExpiredApprovalsJob : ErpJob - Archive old records daily
integrationPoints: WebVella.Erp.Jobs.ErpJob - Base class for background jobs
technicalDetails.technicalApproach: Create job classes inheriting from ErpJob with Execute(JobContext context) override. ProcessApprovalNotificationsJob queries approval_request WHERE status='Pending' AND (last_notification_sent IS NULL OR last_notification_sent < @threshold), resolves approvers via RouteService, creates email entity records for Mail plugin processing, then updates last_notification_sent. ProcessApprovalEscalationsJob queries requests with created_on older than step timeout, calls RouteService.EvaluateNextStep() for escalation target, updates request, and logs via HistoryService. CleanupExpiredApprovalsJob queries completed requests older than retention period and sets is_archived=true. SetSchedulePlans() creates SchedulePlan entries with appropriate Type (Interval or Daily) and interval/time configuration.

dependencies.description: Hooks Integration - Post-update hooks complement notification job for real-time notifications

stories.title: Approval REST API Endpoints
stories.description: Implement the REST API layer for the approval workflow system through ApprovalController, exposing HTTP endpoints for workflow management (CRUD), request actions (approve, reject, delegate), and queries (pending approvals, request details, history). The controller follows WebVella ERP patterns with [Authorize] attribute for authentication, ResponseModel envelope for responses, and service layer delegation for business logic. Includes DTO classes for request body validation.
stories.businessValue: Enables external system integration for approval workflow automation. Provides the data layer for UI components (STORY-008). Consistent ResponseModel envelope simplifies client-side error handling. Centralized authentication ensures security across all approval operations. Well-documented API contracts reduce integration development time.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/v3.0/p/approval/workflow returns list of workflows with optional ?entityName filter
GET /api/v3.0/p/approval/workflow/{id} returns single workflow with optional ?includeStepsAndRules=true parameter
POST /api/v3.0/p/approval/workflow creates new workflow from {name, targetEntity} JSON body
PUT /api/v3.0/p/approval/workflow/{id} updates workflow fields from JSON body
DELETE /api/v3.0/p/approval/workflow/{id} deletes workflow after validating no pending requests
POST /api/v3.0/p/approval/request/{id}/approve processes approval with {comments} body
POST /api/v3.0/p/approval/request/{id}/reject processes rejection with {comments} body
POST /api/v3.0/p/approval/request/{id}/delegate delegates to {delegateToUserId, comments}
GET /api/v3.0/p/approval/pending returns current user's pending approvals with pagination
GET /api/v3.0/p/approval/request/{id} returns request details including history
All endpoints require authentication and return ResponseModel with success, message, object fields
Permission failures return HTTP 403 with descriptive error message

classes: ApprovalController : Controller - REST API controller with [Authorize] attribute
CreateWorkflowModel - DTO for workflow creation
UpdateWorkflowModel - DTO for workflow updates
ApproveRequestModel - DTO for approve/reject actions
DelegateRequestModel - DTO for delegation requests
technicalDetails.technicalApproach: Create ApprovalController class decorated with [Authorize] and injecting IErpService. Implement CurrentUserId property extracting user ID from ClaimTypes.NameIdentifier. Each endpoint method instantiates appropriate service class, calls service method, wraps result in ResponseModel. Error handling uses try-catch with UnauthorizedAccessException returning 403, InvalidOperationException for validation errors, generic Exception for unexpected errors. DTO classes use simple properties with validation in controller methods. Route pattern follows /api/v3.0/p/approval/{resource}/{action} convention matching existing WebVella API structure.

dependencies.description: Service Layer - API endpoints delegate to ApprovalWorkflowService, ApprovalRequestService, ApprovalHistoryService

stories.title: Approval UI Page Components
stories.description: Implement the user interface layer through four page components: PcApprovalWorkflowConfig (admin workflow configuration), PcApprovalRequestList (approval request dashboard), PcApprovalAction (approve/reject/delegate buttons), and PcApprovalHistory (audit timeline display). Components follow the WebVella ERP PageComponent pattern with Design, Display, Options, Help, Error views and client-side service.js for AJAX interactions. All components consume the ApprovalController REST API (STORY-007) for data operations.
stories.businessValue: Provides user-friendly interfaces for approval workflow configuration and processing. Visual workflow configuration enables business users to modify approval rules without developer involvement. Centralized request dashboard improves approver efficiency. History timeline supports audit requirements and troubleshooting. Component architecture enables flexible page layouts matching organizational needs.
acceptanceCriteria: PcApprovalWorkflowConfig renders workflow configuration form with name, target entity dropdown, is_enabled toggle, and Save/Delete buttons
PcApprovalWorkflowConfig Options panel provides workflow_id, show_create_button, entity_filter configuration
PcApprovalRequestList renders paginated table with Request ID, Entity, Requested By, Date, Status, Actions columns
PcApprovalRequestList Options panel provides filter (pending/all/submitted), page_size, show_entity_column, enable_inline_actions configuration
PcApprovalAction renders Approve, Reject, Delegate buttons that trigger confirmation dialogs with comments input
PcApprovalAction Options panel provides request_id, show_approve, show_reject, show_delegate, button_size configuration
PcApprovalHistory renders timeline of approval actions with actor, timestamp, action type, and comments
PcApprovalHistory Options panel provides request_id, show_avatars, relative_time, max_items, sort_order configuration
All components register with PageComponentLibraryService under 'Approval Workflow' category
All components provide Help.cshtml with usage documentation
Client-side service.js handles AJAX calls to ApprovalController with toastr notifications for success/error

classes: PcApprovalWorkflowConfig : PageComponent - Workflow configuration form
PcApprovalWorkflowConfigOptions - Options model for workflow config
PcApprovalRequestList : PageComponent - Request dashboard table
PcApprovalRequestListOptions - Options model for request list
PcApprovalAction : PageComponent - Action buttons component
PcApprovalActionOptions - Options model for action buttons
PcApprovalHistory : PageComponent - History timeline component
PcApprovalHistoryOptions - Options model for history display
integrationPoints: WebVella.Erp.Api.SecurityContext - Current user for authorization display
technicalDetails.technicalApproach: Create component classes inheriting from PageComponent with [PageComponent] attribute specifying Label, Library, Description, Version, IconClass, Category='Approval Workflow'. Each component defines nested Options class with [JsonProperty] decorated properties. InvokeAsync() deserializes options, populates ViewBag, and returns appropriate view based on ComponentMode (Display, Design, Options, Help, Error). Views use Razor with wv-* tag helpers for form fields. service.js uses jQuery AJAX to call ApprovalController endpoints, displaying toastr notifications for results. Design.cshtml shows preview with placeholder data, Display.cshtml loads real data via AJAX, Options.cshtml provides configuration form, Help.cshtml documents component usage.

dependencies.description: REST API - Components consume ApprovalController endpoints for all data operations

stories.title: Manager Approval Dashboard with Real-Time Metrics
stories.description: Implement a real-time dashboard page component (PcApprovalDashboard) that displays team approval workflow metrics for managers, enabling data-driven decision-making and proactive workflow management. The dashboard provides key performance indicators including pending approvals count, average approval processing time, approval rate percentage, overdue requests count, and recent approval activity timeline. The component features auto-refresh capability with a configurable interval (default 60 seconds) and date range filtering for historical analysis. Following the WebVella ERP PageComponent pattern established in STORY-008, the dashboard includes Design, Display, Options, Help, and Error views with a client-side service.js for AJAX interactions. The component consumes the ApprovalController REST API (STORY-007) through a new metrics endpoint that aggregates data from approval_request and approval_history entities. Role-based access control ensures only users with Manager role can view the dashboard metrics, protecting sensitive team performance data.
stories.businessValue: Reduces manager time gathering performance data from multiple sources by providing a unified dashboard view. Enables proactive identification of workflow bottlenecks before escalation through real-time visibility into approval queue health. Provides visibility into team workload for resource planning decisions and capacity management. Supports compliance reporting with real-time SLA monitoring and overdue request tracking. Improves manager accountability through transparent metrics and audit-ready performance data.
acceptanceCriteria: Given I am logged in as a user with Manager role, when I navigate to the Approvals Dashboard page, then I see a dashboard displaying my team's approval metrics including pending approvals count, average processing time, approval rate percentage, overdue requests count, and recent activity
Given the dashboard is displayed, when 60 seconds have elapsed (or the configured refresh interval), then the metrics automatically refresh without requiring a page reload and display updated values

Given I am viewing the dashboard, when I select a date range filter (7 days, 30 days, 90 days, or custom), then the metrics update to reflect only the approval activity within the selected time period
Given I have pending approval requests in my queue, when I view the Pending Approvals metric on the dashboard, then the count accurately reflects the number of requests currently awaiting my action

Given approval requests exist that exceed their configured step timeout (timeout_hours), when I view the Overdue Requests metric, then the count accurately identifies all requests that have passed their SLA threshold
Given I am a user without Manager role, when I attempt to access the Approvals Dashboard page, then I receive an access denied message and cannot view the dashboard metrics

classes: PcApprovalDashboard : PageComponent - Dashboard component with metrics display, auto-refresh timer, and date range filtering
PcApprovalDashboardOptions - Options model with refresh_interval, date_range_default, show_overdue_alert, metrics_to_display properties
DashboardMetricsService - Service for calculating dashboard metrics from approval_request and approval_history entities
DashboardMetricsModel - Response model containing pendingCount, averageProcessingTime, approvalRate, overdueCount, recentActivity properties
integrationPoints: WebVella.Erp.Database.DbContext - EQL queries for metrics aggregation
approval_request entity - Source for pending count, overdue count, status metrics
approval_history entity - Source for processing time calculations and recent activity
WebVella.Erp.Api.SecurityContext - CurrentUser role validation for Manager access control
WebVella.Erp.Web.Models.PageComponent - Base class for component implementation
technicalDetails.technicalApproach: Create PcApprovalDashboard component following the established PageComponent pattern from STORY-008. The component class inherits from PageComponent with [PageComponent] attribute specifying Category='Approval Workflow'. InvokeAsync() method checks user role via SecurityContext.CurrentUser before rendering. Display.cshtml renders Bootstrap card layout with metric widgets and JavaScript timer for auto-refresh. Options.cshtml provides configuration for refresh_interval (number field, default 60), date_range_default (dropdown: 7d/30d/90d), show_overdue_alert (checkbox), and metrics_to_display (multi-select). service.js implements AJAX polling using setInterval() calling the metrics endpoint, with jQuery DOM updates for metric values. DashboardMetricsService uses EQL queries with aggregation: pending count via WHERE status='Pending' AND current_step approver matches current user, average processing time via AVG of timestamp differences in approval_history, approval rate via COUNT with status grouping, overdue count via timestamp comparison against approval_step.timeout_hours. ApprovalController extended with GET /api/v3.0/p/approval/dashboard/metrics endpoint accepting optional from/to date parameters, returning DashboardMetricsModel JSON response.

dependencies.description: REST API - Dashboard component consumes ApprovalController endpoints for metrics data retrieval
UI Components - Dashboard follows the PageComponent pattern established by approval UI components
